realism_principles: depict a plausible real-world moment, not an abstract symbol board or generic stock office
show believable spatial depth, practical lights, surface imperfections, dust, rain, fingerprints, cables, worn furniture, and issue-specific human movement
anonymous non-identifiable human presence is allowed only as backs, silhouettes, cropped hands, or out-of-focus press shapes
the main subject should be a real scene that could have been photographed by a documentary camera, while preserving clean overlay lanes without turning the image into an empty background
every generated scene must contain a visible event, active gesture, anonymous adult reaction, or object in motion; empty rooms and passive still-life backgrounds are not acceptable

use only the pipeline-approved short native caption element when text is requested; never add uncontrolled text, real politician faces, party logos, campaign materials, identifiable private people, or fake screenshots
drama_principles: use a dominant foreground object, partial obstruction, hard shadow, reflection, or doorway frame so the scene feels caught mid-event
make the first visual read within one second through silhouette scale, lens compression, strong diagonal lines, and clear object hierarchy

favor the moment just before or just after public accountability pressure: a press line turning tense, a meeting-room reaction, a doorway confrontation, a hand separating evidence, a vacant chair under harsh light
keep civic safety constraints, but do not make the frame polite, empty, or purely atmospheric

visual_treatments.real_scene_reconstruction: mandatory visible action: photorealistic reconstruction of a public-interest moment as if photographed on location, with anonymous people or cropped hands caught mid-action
visual_treatments.satirical_tableau: mandatory visible action: photorealistic editorial satire tableau with exaggerated anonymous archetype adults, awkward civic-room staging, absurd institutional props, dark humor, theatrical blocking, and no real person likeness
visual_treatments.aino_character_scene: mandatory visible character: a fictional AiNo analyst appears waist-up or over-the-shoulder as a composed non-real AI news host inside the scene, wearing a bright clean jacket, reacting to the room tension or pointing toward a scene-integrated caption rail, expressive but not sexualized and not a real person
visual_treatments.accountability_stage: mandatory visible action: public accountability moment in a concrete real-world location, with anonymous adults reacting, someone stepping away, or a question landing in the room; avoid empty symbolic-only staging
visual_treatments.evidence_action: mandatory visible action: evidence-action close-up that stays attached to the real topic location, with cropped hands, real surfaces, phone recorder, receipts, cups, leaflets, or source packets moving through the foreground without becoming a text-on-paper image
visual_treatments.comment_choice_scene: mandatory visible action: comment-choice scene built around a video editor timeline, comment heatmap glow, and a fictional AiNo editor preparing a debate choice without holding paper cards

hook: a door is half-open and the viewer catches the moment something is entering the classroom
an anonymous adult silhouette is frozen in the doorway while another adult turns back toward the tension
why_now: a newsroom monitor glow snaps on while the background staff react
a phone recorder glow reflects on glass as someone leans toward a microphone line
evidence: cropped hands separate one evidence packet while a recorder runs nearby
a red pencil and recorder sit in the foreground while anonymous adults react behind glass
criteria: three table zones are separated by light and object placement at the exact decision moment
a hand moves one object away from the others while two adults watch
responsibility: an anonymous adult steps away from a public counter or meeting table while others react in the background
a question lands in a real room and two anonymous adults turn toward the source of tension
verification: one unresolved item is stopped by a cropped hand before it reaches the confirmed side
rain on glass cuts across two separated civic-room sightlines
cta: a hand hovers over a video timeline and three blurred comment heat zones
the fictional AiNo editor turns toward the viewer beside a glowing timeline and divided reaction wall

default_concrete_scene: a concrete public-interest location tied to the topic, photographed mid-event with anonymous adults reacting, one specific object from the story in the foreground, and no readable text

scenes_by_role.hook: election-night polling analysis room at dawn, anonymous analysts seen from behind, a large blank regional map wall made of colored blocks, a phone recorder and sealed blank poll packet in the foreground, no party colors as logos and no readable numbers

scenes_by_role.verification: quiet press-room side table after a polling briefing, unbranded microphones low in the foreground, sealed blank survey envelopes stopped by a cropped hand before joining the confirmed pile
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor at a video timeline showing polling call-center, blank map wall, and counting-table thumbnails, with three blurred comment heat zones reflected on the monitor

open classroom doorway blurred in the distance

verification.location_suffix: second verification beat in a different public archive counter, not another rainy desk
verification.camera_suffix: side-angle shot across a glass service counter with a cropped stamp pad and a single sealed envelope
verification.palette_suffix: pale archive white, cool teal glass, single amber counter edge, no green room dominance
anchors: glass public archive counter
cropped anonymous clerk hand behind frosted glass
sealed blank envelope beside an unbranded stamp pad

verification.location_suffix: third verification beat in an empty press room corridor outside a briefing door, not an editor desk
verification.camera_suffix: long lens down a narrow corridor with a blank briefing door and recorder silhouettes in foreground
verification.palette_suffix: cold hallway gray with restrained blue recorder lights
anchors: blank briefing room door
cluster of unbranded audio recorders in foreground
anonymous shadows waiting outside the room

criteria.location_suffix: alternate criteria beat at a wall-mounted evidence timeline, not another tabletop
criteria.camera_suffix: three-quarter shot with shallow focus across pinned blank cards and colored string
criteria.palette_suffix: paper white, muted teal wall, small red thread accent
anchors: wall-mounted blank timeline cards
colored string crossing the frame
cropped hand holding a blank note

hook: wet public building steps or memorial plaza edge
press camera silhouettes behind a rope line
umbrella edge or anonymous shoulder crossing the foreground diagonally
evidence: single overhead verification table with three light-separated zones
small audio recorder beside a red pencil
cropped hand separating one object from another
worn table scratches and glass reflections visible
cta: warm video editing desk with divided comment heat zones
pen hovering beside a glowing edit timeline
soft timeline glow with no visible letters or numbers
coffee cup and lamp creating a warm foreground frame
why_now: muted phone notification glow with no readable UI
blank calendar grid turned away from the camera
responsibility: empty public hearing room with a vacant witness chair
unbranded microphone row in the foreground
hard overhead cone of light on one empty seat
dark public gallery silhouettes without faces
criteria: two clearly separated light zones on a bright table
cropped hand moving one marker between sides
cyan sticky marker with no writing
phone recorder and red pencil placed as scale objects
verification: two separated verification zones split by lamp shadow
unreadable labels turned away from camera
unresolved side stopped by a cropped hand

safety_constraints: no signs, placards, banners, protest cards, printed slogans, visible letters, or readable marks inside the generated image
no readable text, letters, signs, labels, numbers, or screenshots
blank paper must stay blank; do not invent Korean, English, symbols, dates, titles, or fake handwriting

replacement_safety_constraints: only the exact approved short Korean native caption may be readable
no extra words, outlet names, logos, seals, dates, document numbers, UI screenshots, random glyphs, or fake handwriting

role_surfaces.hook: a scene-integrated lower-third caption strip formed by light on glass or a shallow acrylic news caption rail, not a handheld paper card
role_surfaces.why_now: a small in-scene newsroom monitor caption rail or glass reflection caption, not a phone screenshot or paper cue card
role_surfaces.evidence: one projected caption strip on an evidence-room wall or monitor bezel, not a source card or desk paper
role_surfaces.criteria: a clean caption band integrated into the table edge or glass divider, not sorting cards
role_surfaces.responsibility: a caption strip on a meeting-room glass wall or table-edge display, not a placard or memo
role_surfaces.verification: a reflection-style fact-check caption on rain glass or monitor edge, not a sticky note
role_surfaces.cta: a short editor-room caption panel near the video timeline, not a comment card
role_surfaces.default: one scene-integrated caption strip inside the photographed environment, not a paper card

diegetic_text.directive_template: Controlled in-image text: include exactly one readable native caption element, {surface}, with the exact Korean text "{text}". The real situation, anonymous people, location depth, and active gesture must remain the primary subject; the caption is secondary but legible. Do not put the text on a handheld paper, placard, protest sign, memo, document, sticky note, poster, or comment card. Do not render a fake app screenshot or broadcast UI. Keep the approved text sharp, high-contrast, short, centered in the caption element, and physically integrated with light, glass, monitor bezel, table edge, or architectural surface. Do not invent any other readable text, numbers, logos, seals, screenshots, labels, or random glyphs.

prompting.visual_intent_suffix: into a heightened cinematic debate tableau with clear civic conflict, not a symbolic office still

hot_visual.base: photorealistic cinematic Korean editorial satire and investigative debate still
hot_visual.template: {base}, real-world moment: {reality_beat}, foreground tension: {intensity_beat}, {location}, {camera}, required concrete anchors: {anchors}, palette: {palette}, {composition}, {texture}, {people_safety}, {policy_safety}
hot_visual.composition: one dominant foreground object, high-contrast first-second composition
hot_visual.texture: documentary texture, not a generic empty office, no readable text
hot_visual.people_safety: anonymous non-identifiable people only as silhouettes or cropped hands
hot_visual.policy_safety: no identifiable person, no real politician likeness, no party logos, no horror styling, no glowing eyes, no fantasy or non-human figures

cinematic.directed_primary_template: Create this concrete scene first: {concrete_scene}. Scene-integrated caption element: {diegetic_text_directive}. Apply this treatment: {visual_treatment}. Capture this action beat clearly: {action_beat}. Use only a few supporting anchors from this list when they fit the concrete scene: {visual_anchors}. Visually imply these topic terms without writing any other text beyond the approved native caption: {topic_relevance_terms}. Original context to reinterpret, not copy literally: {source_visual}
cinematic.subtitle_prompt_directive: No readable in-image text. Keep all papers, screens, signs, labels, UI, and cards blank, blurred, turned away, or cropped. Korean captions are rendered later by the video subtitle layer.
cinematic.subtitle_primary_template: Create this concrete scene first: {concrete_scene}. Make it a believable public-interest moment with visible action, anonymous adults, real location depth, and a clean lower-third area for later subtitles. Apply this treatment: {visual_treatment}. Capture this action beat clearly: {action_beat}. Use supporting anchors only when they fit: {visual_anchors}. Do not place any readable text inside the generated image.
subtitle_lines: Asset type: vertical TikTok background plate for a Korean political commentary video
Render style: cinematic subtitle plate, not card-news, not a text-on-paper image
Scene-specific priority: the frame must look like a captured real situation tied to the issue, not a symbolic office still-life
Topic relevance terms to imply visually without writing them: {topic_relevance_terms}
Composition/framing: vertical 9:16 portrait, off-center subject, strong foreground object, visible action, clean lower third for subtitles, no empty dead background
Subtitle lane: reserve the lower third as darker or less busy negative space, but still keep it part of the real photographed scene
People rule: anonymous non-identifiable adults only as backs, silhouettes, cropped hands, or fictional AiNo editor if requested; no real politician likeness
Lighting/mood: believable practical lighting, serious investigative tone, filmic depth of field, real surface imperfections
Palette discipline: follow the Palette line exactly. Across the full set, {palette_discipline}.

Palette override rule: if the Palette line contains repeat-variant, the repeat-variant palette is mandatory and must visibly override the base role palette; do not let repeated roles collapse into the same dark green or navy room tone.
Scene uniqueness: this scene must differ by location, camera distance, dominant object, palette, and action beat. Do not reuse the same corridor, desk angle, file stack, or wall composition.

Avoid for photorealistic/reformed_briefing plates: text card, placard, poster, propaganda image, real-person caricature, fake screenshot, fake broadcast lower third, watermark, party logo, extra text, distorted hands, generic stock photo. For ranking_battle_65 and narrative_confession only, text card, placard, and poster are allowed when rendered by the controlled_kinetic_still pipeline as original typography cards; fake broadcast UI, party logos, real-person synthesis, and uncontrolled extra text remain forbidden.
lines: Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: vertical TikTok background plate for a public-interest fact-check video
Primary request: {primary_request}
Concrete scene directive: {concrete_scene}
Original visual context: {source_visual}
Scene-specific priority: follow the Concrete scene directive and Primary request first; role language only changes camera tension, not the actual setting
Literal-scene rule: this must feel like a captured moment from the actual public controversy, not a generic symbolic still-life of folders, microphones, empty chairs, or abstract evidence props.

Treatment override: when the Visual treatment is satirical tableau or AiNo character scene, the treatment and action beat should dominate the literal background request while preserving the issue context
Director note: do not simply render the Original visual context as a conservative documentary background. Invent a cinematic, character-driven, event-in-progress scene from the Primary request.
Visual treatment: {visual_treatment}
Action beat: {action_beat}
Visual intent: {visual_intent}
Real-world moment: {reality_beat}
Foreground tension: {intensity_beat}

Thumbnail drama: for hook and why-now cards, build a high-contrast first-second composition with one dominant foreground object, compressed depth, and a clear visual question before any overlay text appears
Supporting visual anchors, lower priority than the concrete scene: {visual_anchors}
Issue type: {issue_type}
Card role: {role}
Location: {location}
Camera: {camera}
Emotion: {emotion}
Palette: {palette}
Documentary realism principles: {realism_principles}
Drama principles: {drama_principles}
Topic relevance terms to imply visually, except for the controlled native caption element: {topic_relevance_terms}

Scene/backdrop: realistic South Korean civic, school, newsroom, cafe, public-office, or street environment that matches the Concrete scene directive, editorial documentary mood, no staged stock-photo feeling, no abstract symbolic-only composition

Style/medium: photorealistic cinematic film still or photorealistic editorial satire when the treatment requires it, 35mm documentary lens, natural imperfect texture, subtle film grain, real photographed surfaces

Composition/framing: vertical 9:16 portrait composition, subject placed off-center, one dominant foreground object, strong overlay lanes in upper and lower thirds without empty dead background, plus one secondary scene-integrated native caption element when requested
{diegetic_text_directive}

Text legibility priority: the approved Korean native caption must be readable in the generated image, but it must not become a handheld paper card, memo, placard, sticky note, poster, or protest sign.
Character/satire rule: if a character appears, make them fictional, anonymous, expressive, and non-identifiable; satire should come from staging and props, not from real faces or party symbols
Liveliness rule: do not output an empty classroom, empty office, passive desk still-life, or generic background plate. The image must show a visible event or active human gesture.

Lighting/mood: believable practical lighting, serious investigative tone, filmic depth of field, with brightness and color temperature matching the palette instead of defaulting to all-dark navy/black
Palette discipline: follow the Palette line exactly. Across the full card set, {palette_discipline}.

Palette override rule: if the Palette line contains repeat-variant, the repeat-variant palette is mandatory and must visibly override the base role palette; do not let repeated roles collapse into the same dark green or navy room tone.
Constraints: {safety_constraints}
If documents, posters, screens, or building signs appear: keep every surface blank, turned away, cropped out, or too blurred to read except the single approved native caption element.
If a national flag appears: it must be a small physical object in the scene, not a party/campaign symbol and not a propaganda poster.

Scene uniqueness: this scene must be visually distinct from the other cards by location, camera distance, palette, and dominant object. Do not reuse the same corridor, desk angle, file stack, or wall composition across cards.
Repetition guard: {repetition_guard}
Role separation: {role_separation}
Must not be a generic empty office or a flat metaphor. The concrete scene should make this specific card understandable as a real photographed scene even before overlay text appears.
Avoid: propaganda poster, real-person caricature, fantasy illustration, fake news screenshot, distorted hands, extra unapproved text, over-saturated colors, glossy corporate stock image

codex_cli.style: photorealistic cinematic editorial satire, character-driven public-interest scene reconstruction
codex_cli.composition: vertical 9:16 portrait, strong overlay lanes for Korean overlay, visible action beat, one expressive focal character or prop
codex_cli.lighting: moody practical light, serious investigative tone with satirical edge when requested
codex_cli.constraints: no real politician likeness, no party logos, only the approved short native Korean caption element may be readable, no watermark, no identifiable children, no horror styling, no glowing eyes, no fantasy or non-human figures
codex_cli.negative: propaganda poster, real-person caricature, fake screenshot, unapproved overlaid text, large blank white board, blank paper panel, people holding blank cards toward camera, placard wall, distorted hands, celebrity face, horror lighting, glowing eyes, fantasy figure

paper_prop_regression_terms: approved text prop is a primary foreground subject
stamped-looking but fully fictional source card
plain meeting-room placard or response memo
no extra readable text except the approved diegetic text prop

source_visual_replacements.no extra readable text except the approv: only the approved native caption element may be readable
source_visual_replacements.a folder crossing the lens: an anonymous shoulder crossing the lens
source_visual_replacements.public records counter with cropped hand: public records counter with cropped hands separating two verification zones
source_visual_replacements.campaign-frame cards turned away from ca: split reaction zones and body-language tension
source_visual_replacements.blank campaign narrative cards: monitor thumbnails and divided reaction zones

repetition_guard: do not make every scene a dark desk, file pile, paper card, placard, sticky note, document close-up, or comment-card setup. At most two scenes in a 9-card set may use documents as the dominant foreground. Prefer public rooms, corridors, press lines, monitor glow, anonymous reactions, civic space depth, and real situation changes.
role_separation: why_now must look like a newsroom/current-signal moment, CTA must look like an editor timeline and divided reaction wall, not a paper-card desk or repeated corridor. Criteria must look like a comparison decision scene, not a hallway. Responsibility must look like an accountability room or issue-specific public meeting space, not an evidence desk.
